name: ObjectNumber
description: Displays a numeric value with a unit of measure — amounts, quantities, prices, counts. The number and unit are visually paired and can carry semantic state. Always use ObjectNumber instead of plain Text for numeric values that have a unit (EUR, kg, pcs). Supports right-aligned number display for monetary columns.

props.name: number
props.description: The numeric value as a formatted string. E.g. '1,250.00'. Use formatted strings (not raw numbers) to respect locale-specific number formatting.

props.name: unit
props.description: The unit of measure shown after the number. E.g. 'EUR', 'kg', 'pcs', '%'. Displayed as a smaller secondary text next to the number.

props.name: state
props.description: Semantic color state. Use for overdue amounts (Error/Warning), approved amounts (Success). None = neutral black.

props.name: emphasized
props.description: When true, the number is displayed in bold. Use for totals or key financial figures that need visual emphasis.

props.name: textDirection
props.description: Text direction for the number. Numbers should always be LTR even in RTL layouts — set explicitly when needed.

notes: Always use ObjectNumber for monetary amounts, quantities, and measurements — never use sap.m.Text.
Always set both number and unit — a number without a unit is ambiguous.
Right-align amount columns by setting hAlign='End' on the parent sap.m.Column.
Use state=Error for overdue/negative values, state=Warning for near-threshold values.
Format the number string according to locale before passing it (e.g. '1,250.00' not 1250).
Screen readers announce both the number and the unit — ensure both are set.
For monetary columns, ensure the column header names the currency context.
In RTL layouts, numbers should remain LTR — set textDirection='LTR' explicitly.

examples.title: Monetary amount in a table cell
whenToUse: Amount columns in List Report and Worklist tables (order value, invoice total)
Quantity columns (stock count, line item quantity)
KPI figures in Object Page header content area
Any numeric value that has a unit of measure
whenNotToUse: Do not use sap.m.Text for monetary or quantity values — use ObjectNumber
Do not omit the unit — a bare number is ambiguous to the user
